meta.total_spans_on_branch_note: Corrected from 48 (which included working-directory-only changes from partial files). Sum of per-file spans in per-file-evaluation.json = 38.

NDS-001.evidence: All files pass node --check. Tracer ReferenceError in summary-graph.js/sensitive-filter.js is a runtime error, not syntax.

NDS-002.evidence: 32 test failures (ReferenceError: tracer is not defined). Caused by summary-graph.js (21 failures) and sensitive-filter.js (11 failures). Function-level fallback added tracer.startActiveSpan() without tracer import.
NDS-002.note: The failing code was never committed to the branch — the branch contains only passing instrumentation. The test failures occurred in the working directory during the run. The deliverable (branch) would pass NDS-002; the run process does not.

NDS-003.evidence: All 16 instrumented files on the branch have instrumentation-only changes. No non-instrumentation lines modified.
NDS-003.note: Per-file quality evaluation flags NDS-003 for summarize.js and summary-manager.js (catch block semantic changes), but these are instrumentation additions that change behavior — better classified as NDS-005 failures. The gate (only instrumentation lines changed) passes.

API-001.evidence: All 16 source files import only from @opentelemetry/api. No SDK, exporter, or instrumentation package imports.

NDS-006.evidence: All agent-added imports use ESM syntax (import...from). No require() or module.exports. Project is ESM (type: module).

gates.verdict: NDS-002 FAIL blocks a clean gate pass. However, the branch itself is clean — quality scoring proceeds because the deliverable contains only passing instrumentation.

NDS-004.evidence: All 16 instrumented files preserve all exported function signatures. startActiveSpan callbacks wrap function bodies without modifying parameter lists, return types, or export declarations.

NDS-005.evidence: 3 files have expected-condition catch blocks (ENOENT file/directory not found) changed to record exceptions and set ERROR status. OTel setStatus is a one-way latch — spans are permanently ERROR even when the function succeeds. This is a semantic behavioral change to error handling.
NDS-005.failure_class: expected-condition catch block — distinct from traditional NDS-005 (agent broke error handling). See orb issue #10.

COV-001.evidence: index.js main() (CLI entry point, root span) has NO span. Only summarize and journal-generate code paths within main are spanned. Without a root span, the trace has no top-level operation.
COV-001.note: In run-3, index.js had a commit_story.generate_journal_entry span covering main(). Run-4 has spans on code paths within main but not on main() itself — a genuine regression.

COV-002.evidence: claude-collector: findJSONLFiles (readdirSync) and parseJSONLFile (readFileSync) lack individual spans. index.js: 3 git validation calls (isGitRepository, isValidCommitRef, getPreviousCommitTime) lack spans.
COV-002.methodology_note: Run-3 accepted parent span coverage for internal helpers (COV-002 PASS). Run-4 per-file agents applied stricter per-operation evaluation. This is a methodology difference, not necessarily a regression.

COV-003.evidence: All 48 spans across 16 files have recordException + setStatus(ERROR) in catch blocks. Standard error recording pattern followed consistently.

COV-004.evidence: claude-collector: findJSONLFiles/parseJSONLFile are I/O operations without spans. git-collector: getCommitMetadata/getCommitDiff/getMergeInfo are async without individual spans (parent covers them). index.js: main() is async without a span.
COV-004.methodology_note: Run-3 accepted parent span coverage (COV-004 PASS). Run-4 evaluates individual operations more strictly.

COV-005.evidence: auto-summarize (4 ad-hoc attrs) and summary-detector (3 ad-hoc attrs) use attributes not in the Weaver registry. These are schema-uncovered summary subsystem files where no registry definitions exist.
COV-005.schema_coverage_note: Both failing files are schema-uncovered. The ad-hoc attributes follow commit_story.* namespace and are semantically valid. Under schema coverage split methodology, this would be evaluated differently (see schema_coverage_split section).

COV-006.evidence: journal-graph.js: manual spans on LangGraph graph nodes are justified — @traceloop/instrumentation-langchain covers ChatAnthropic.invoke() but NOT LangGraph node execution, state transitions, or graph orchestration.

RST-001.evidence: truncateDiff() and truncateMessages() are exported but are pure synchronous data transformation functions with no I/O. They should not have their own spans — they are called from applyTokenBudget (which has a span). Adding spans to pure functions is over-instrumentation.

RST-002.evidence: No spans on trivial accessors or property getters across all files.

RST-003.evidence: No duplicate spans on thin wrappers.

RST-004.evidence: All spanned unexported functions have I/O exemption. 2 borderline cases in MCP tools (saveContext, saveReflection — unexported but do I/O, exemption applies).

RST-005.evidence: No pre-existing instrumentation. First instrumentation run on this codebase.

API-002.evidence: @opentelemetry/api is in peerDependencies (^1.9.0). commit-story-v2 is a library — peerDependencies is correct. The agent preserved the existing declaration.
API-002.run3_comparison: IMPROVEMENT from run-3 FAIL (agent marked @opentelemetry/api as optional).

API-003.evidence: No vendor-specific SDK packages. @traceloop/instrumentation-langchain and @traceloop/instrumentation-mcp added as optional peerDependencies — community auto-instrumentation, not vendor SDKs.
API-003.run3_comparison: IMPROVEMENT from run-3 FAIL (stale build included @traceloop/node-server-sdk mega-bundle).

API-004.evidence: No @opentelemetry/sdk-* or instrumentation-* imports in source files. All OTel imports resolve to @opentelemetry/api only.

SCH-001.evidence: 8 of 37 span names (22%) deviate from the commit_story.* convention: context.gather_for_commit, summary.daily/weekly/monthly.generate, mcp.server.start, context.capture.save, mcp.tool.journal_capture_context, mcp.tool.journal_add_reflection. The remaining 29/37 (78%) use commit_story.* consistently.
SCH-001.note: Deviation correlates with file processing order — later files deviate more. Schema evolution (when fixed) would propagate naming conventions across files.

SCH-002.evidence: 11 ad-hoc attribute keys not in registry across 3 files. All follow commit_story.* namespace convention and represent legitimate domain concepts.
SCH-002.note: All 11 ad-hoc attributes are semantically valid, follow namespace conventions, and would be reasonable registry additions. Schema evolution mechanism (broken in run-4) would have registered these automatically.

SCH-003.evidence: All attribute values conform to registry type constraints. Enum values valid, integers for counts, ISO strings for timestamps, booleans for flags.

SCH-004.evidence: No redundant or duplicate attributes. Ad-hoc attributes capture genuinely new domain concepts not covered by existing registry entries.

CDQ-001.evidence: All 48 spans use startActiveSpan callback pattern with finally { span.end() }. No span leaks possible.

CDQ-002.evidence: All 16 files use trace.getTracer('unknown_service'). The library name should be 'commit-story' (from package.json#name). This is a systemic agent configuration bug — one root cause, 16 affected files.
CDQ-002.note: Run-3 CDQ-002 evaluated only whether trace.getTracer() was called correctly (pattern check) and PASSED. Run-4 evaluates whether the library name is correct (semantic check). This is a stricter criterion applied to CDQ-002 in run-4. The underlying bug (wrong tracer name) existed in run-3 but was captured by CDQ-008 (inconsistent naming) instead.

CDQ-003.evidence: summarize.js uses recordException + setStatus(ERROR) on a non-error control flow path — file-not-found is the expected condition for generating a new summary. Misuse of the error recording pattern.
CDQ-003.run3_comparison: Run-3 CDQ-003 failed for commit-analyzer.js (missing recordException). That file passes in run-4. New failure in summarize.js is a different issue (misuse vs absence).

CDQ-005.evidence: All spans use startActiveSpan callback pattern which automatically maintains async context. No manual context.with() needed.

CDQ-006.evidence: toISOString() calls without span.isRecording() guard in 3 sites across 2 files.
CDQ-006.methodology_note: Run-3 CDQ-006 PASSED because toISOString() was deemed lightweight. Run-4 per-file agents applied the rubric strictly — any attribute computation without isRecording() guard is a CDQ-006 violation. Whether toISOString() requires a guard is debatable — it's a very cheap operation.

CDQ-007.evidence: No unbounded or PII attributes. commit_story.commit.author is in registry with PII annotation (accepted per decision log 2026-03-15). All attribute values are bounded.
CDQ-007.run3_comparison: IMPROVEMENT from run-3 FAIL (CDQ-007 flagged commit_story.commit.author as PII). Run-4 passes because PII annotation was added to registry — this is a schema/rubric change, not an agent improvement.

CDQ-008.evidence: All 16 files use trace.getTracer('unknown_service'). Single naming convention — consistent. The name is wrong (should be 'commit-story'), but CDQ-008 evaluates consistency, not correctness.
CDQ-008.run3_comparison: IMPROVEMENT from run-3 FAIL (two naming conventions: commit_story and commit-story). Fresh build resolved the stale-build inconsistency.

NDS.note: NDS-005 regression: 3 files have expected-condition catch blocks changed to record exceptions. New failure class not seen in run-3.

COV.note: Major apparent regression, but largely driven by: (1) stricter per-file agent evaluation vs run-3 single-pass (COV-002, COV-004), (2) new summary subsystem files with no registry coverage (COV-005), (3) genuine regression on index.js root span (COV-001). See methodology_adjusted for context.

methodology_adjusted.COV-002: Would PASS under run-3 methodology (parent span coverage accepted)
methodology_adjusted.COV-004: Would PASS under run-3 methodology (parent span coverage accepted)
methodology_adjusted.adjusted_score: 4/6 (67%) — still lower than run-3 due to genuine COV-001 and COV-005 failures

RST.note: RST-001 regression: token-filter.js has spans on pure synchronous functions.

API.note: API-002 fixed (agent no longer marks @opentelemetry/api optional). API-003 fixed (fresh build eliminated mega-bundle). API-004 remains passing. The largest single-dimension improvement in run-4.

SCH.note: Same score but different failure profiles. Run-3: 4+ naming patterns (stale build) and 2 ad-hoc attrs. Run-4: naming deviation on 8/37 spans (schema-uncovered files) and 11 ad-hoc attrs (all in schema-uncovered files). Schema coverage split reinterprets these failures.

CDQ.note: Same score, completely different failure set. Run-3 failures: CDQ-003 (missing recordException), CDQ-007 (PII), CDQ-008 (inconsistent naming). Run-4 failures: CDQ-002 (wrong tracer name), CDQ-003 (misuse of error recording), CDQ-006 (missing isRecording guard). Run-3 failures resolved (fresh build, schema change); new failures appeared.

methodology_adjusted.note: Adjusts COV-002 and COV-004 to PASS (run-3 methodology accepted parent span coverage) and CDQ-002 to PASS (run-3 methodology checked pattern, not name correctness).

schema_coverage_split.note: Applies split scoring to SCH-002 (schema-uncovered files evaluated on invention quality rather than registry match). SCH-002 passes under split methodology.

methodology_adjusted_plus_split.note: Combines methodology adjustment (COV-002, COV-004, CDQ-002) and schema coverage split (SCH-002). Most favorable interpretation.

failure_classification.evidence: 32 test failures from missing tracer imports in function-level fallback. Never committed to branch — branch is clean.
3 files: expected-condition catch blocks (ENOENT) changed to record exceptions + ERROR status. New failure class: agent doesn't distinguish error handling from expected-condition control flow.
index.js missing root span on main(). Run-3 had a root span. This is a genuine reduction in coverage for the CLI entry point.
Run-3 accepted parent span coverage; run-4 per-file agents evaluate individual operations. Not a genuine regression.
Same as COV-002 — stricter evaluation of individual async operations.
Ad-hoc attributes in schema-uncovered summary files. The agent invented valid attributes in a domain with no registry guidance. Partially addressed by schema coverage split.
token-filter.js: spans on pure synchronous functions (truncateDiff, truncateMessages). Over-instrumentation.
8/37 span names deviate from commit_story.* convention. Deviating names are in files processed later in the run. Schema evolution (broken) would propagate naming conventions.
11 ad-hoc attributes in 3 schema-uncovered files. All follow namespace conventions. Schema evolution would have registered these. Schema coverage split reinterprets this.
All 16 files: trace.getTracer('unknown_service') instead of 'commit-story'. Systemic agent bug — one root cause.
failure_classification.note: Run-3 CDQ-002 used a pattern-only check (PASS). Run-4 checks name correctness (FAIL). The bug existed in run-3 but wasn't captured by CDQ-002.

failure_classification.evidence: summarize.js: recordException + ERROR status on expected control flow (file-not-found). Misuse of error recording pattern.
toISOString() without isRecording() guard. Run-3 deemed this lightweight (PASS). Run-4 applies strict rubric interpretation (FAIL). Debatable whether toISOString() requires a guard.

schema_coverage_split.methodology: Score SCH-001 and SCH-002 separately for schema-covered files (~10 instrumented, where registry defines expected attributes) vs schema-uncovered files (~6 instrumented, summary subsystem files where agent must invent attributes).

SCH-001.strict_evidence: 8/37 span names deviate from commit_story.* (22% deviation)
SCH-001.covered_evidence: context-integrator.js uses 'context.gather_for_commit' instead of 'commit_story.context.gather_for_commit'. 1 of 10 covered files deviates (10%). This is a genuine naming failure — the registry defines commit_story.* convention.
SCH-001.uncovered_evidence: summary-manager uses summary.* prefix (should be commit_story.summary.*). MCP files use mcp.* and context.* prefixes. 4 of 6 uncovered files deviate. However, some deviation is expected without schema evolution to propagate conventions.
SCH-001.split_interpretation: SCH-001 fails under both strict and split scoring. The covered file failure (context-integrator) is a genuine bug. The uncovered file failures are partially explained by broken schema evolution but indicate the agent doesn't default to commit_story.* even without guidance.

SCH-002.strict_evidence: 11 ad-hoc attribute keys not in registry across 3 files
SCH-002.covered_evidence: All 10 schema-covered files use only registry-defined attributes. 0 ad-hoc attributes in covered files.
SCH-002.uncovered_evidence: All 11 ad-hoc attributes follow commit_story.* namespace, represent legitimate domain concepts, use appropriate types, and would be reasonable registry additions. Invention quality is high.
SCH-002.split_interpretation: SCH-002 PASSES under split methodology. Schema-covered files are fully compliant. Schema-uncovered files show good attribute invention quality. The strict failure is entirely attributable to the schema gap (by design — see decision log 2026-03-16).

ad_hoc_quality_assessment.naming_convention: 10/11 (91%) use consistent snake_case — commit_story.summarize.force (boolean) could arguably be commit_story.summarize.is_forced for consistency
ad_hoc_quality_assessment.type_appropriateness: 11/11 (100%) use correct types (integers for counts, boolean for flag)

run3_to_run4_improvements.reason: Agent no longer marks @opentelemetry/api as optional
PII annotation added to registry (schema change, not agent improvement)
Fresh build resolved inconsistent tracer naming

run3_to_run4_regressions.reason: Expected-condition catch blocks changed in 3 new summary files
Stricter criterion — run-3 checked pattern only; run-4 checks name correctness

run3_to_run4_methodology_changes.reason: Stricter per-file evaluation (parent span coverage no longer sufficient)
New summary files with ad-hoc attributes (new territory)
toISOString() now requires isRecording() guard (stricter interpretation)

run3_to_run4_unchanged_failures.note: Different failure profile: run-3 had 4+ naming patterns (stale build); run-4 has 8/37 deviations (schema evolution)
Different failure profile: run-3 had 2 ad-hoc attrs; run-4 has 11 (all in new files)
Different files: run-3 commit-analyzer (missing recordException); run-4 summarize.js (misuse on expected condition)

file_rescue_assessment.goal: Rescue at least 2 of 4 persistently failing files

coverage_assessment.note: Run-4 processed 8 more files than run-3. The increase from 11 to 16 instrumented files includes 2 rescued files and 3 new summary subsystem files.
